definition: An airfield (an area designated for the takeoff and landing of aircraft), including one or more runways and (for commercial airports) one or more passenger terminals.
example: We arrived at the airport three hours before our international flight.
exampleVi: Chúng tôi đến sân bay ba giờ trước chuyến bay quốc tế của mình.
collocations: arrive at the airport, airport terminal, international airport

definition: A building in an airport where passengers transfer from ground transportation to the facilities that allow them to board airplanes.
example: Passengers flying with that airline should check in at Terminal 2.
exampleVi: Hành khách bay với hãng đó nên làm thủ tục tại Nhà ga 2.
collocations: airport terminal, departure terminal, terminal building

definition: A journey made by an airplane, especially a scheduled trip between two airports.
example: Our flight to Tokyo was delayed by two hours.
exampleVi: Chuyến bay của chúng tôi đến Tokyo bị hoãn hai giờ.
collocations: book a flight, direct flight, flight delay, connecting flight

definition: An aircraft with fixed wings, powered by engines.
example: The plane took off right on schedule.
exampleVi: Máy bay cất cánh đúng giờ như dự kiến.
collocations: board the plane, catch a plane, plane ticket

definition: A powered heavier-than-air aircraft with fixed wings.
example: The airplane landed safely despite the storm.
exampleVi: Máy bay đã hạ cánh an toàn bất chấp cơn bão.
collocations: board an airplane, airplane seat, airplane mode

definition: A company that flies airplanes to transport people and goods.
example: The airline offers direct flights to over fifty destinations.
exampleVi: Hãng hàng không này cung cấp các chuyến bay thẳng đến hơn năm mươi điểm đến.
collocations: budget airline, airline ticket, major airline

definition: The area in an airport terminal where passengers board their flight.
example: Please proceed to gate 24 for boarding.
exampleVi: Vui lòng đến cổng 24 để lên máy bay.
collocations: departure gate, boarding gate, gate number

definition: A defined, narrow section of land or an artificial structure used for access.
example: The plane taxied to the runway before takeoff.
exampleVi: Máy bay di chuyển ra đường băng trước khi cất cánh.
collocations: clear the runway, runway lights, land on the runway

definition: A document that entitles a passenger to travel on a flight.
example: She booked a round-trip ticket to Paris.
exampleVi: Cô ấy đã đặt một vé khứ hồi đến Paris.
collocations: book a ticket, e-ticket, one-way ticket, ticket counter

definition: To step or climb onto or otherwise enter a ship, aircraft, train or other conveyance.
example: It is time to board the aircraft.
exampleVi: Đã đến lúc lên máy bay.
collocations: boarding time, boarding pass, start boarding

definition: To go through a checkpoint or process successfully, such as security or immigration.
example: It took twenty minutes to pass through security.
exampleVi: Phải mất hai mươi phút để vượt qua khu vực kiểm tra an ninh.
collocations: pass through security, pass inspection, boarding pass

definition: To hand over luggage to an airline for storage in the aircraft's cargo hold.
example: You can check your suitcase at the counter before security.
exampleVi: Bạn có thể ký gửi vali của mình tại quầy trước khi qua an ninh.
collocations: check in luggage, check-in counter, check the flight status

definition: Used to indicate arrival or location within a place, such as an airport or flight.
example: Please arrive in the terminal two hours before departure.
exampleVi: Vui lòng đến nhà ga hai giờ trước khi khởi hành.
collocations: check in, arrive in, land in

definition: The condition of not being threatened, especially physically, psychologically, emotionally, or financially.
example: Passengers must remove their shoes at the security checkpoint.
exampleVi: Hành khách phải cởi giày tại điểm kiểm tra an ninh.
collocations: security checkpoint, airport security, pass through security

definition: (in the plural) The duties or taxes imposed on imported or exported goods.
example: Customs of £200 were due on all the wine we took back from France.
exampleVi: Số tiền hải quan 200 bảng Anh phải nộp đối với tất cả số rượu chúng tôi mang về từ Pháp.
collocations: customs declaration, go through customs, customs officer

definition: (usually uncountable) Luggage; traveling equipment
example: Please put your baggage in the trunk.
exampleVi: Vui lòng để hành lý của bạn vào cốp xe.
collocations: baggage claim, checked baggage, baggage allowance, excess baggage

definition: The area in an airport where passengers collect their checked luggage after a flight.
example: Passengers can pick up their suitcases at baggage claim.
exampleVi: Hành khách có thể lấy vali của mình tại khu vực nhận hành lý.
collocations: baggage claim, claim your luggage, claim area

definition: A period of time before an event occurs; the act of delaying; procrastination; lingering inactivity.
example: Bad weather caused a two-hour delay to our flight.
exampleVi: Thời tiết xấu đã khiến chuyến bay của chúng tôi bị trì hoãn hai giờ.
collocations: flight delay, cause a delay, without delay

definition: To stop a planned flight or booking from happening.
example: The airline canceled the flight due to the snowstorm.
exampleVi: Hãng hàng không đã hủy chuyến bay do bão tuyết.
collocations: cancel a flight, canceled flight, cancellation fee

definition: To bring an aircraft down onto the ground at the end of a flight.
example: The plane is scheduled to land at 6 p.m.
exampleVi: Máy bay dự kiến sẽ hạ cánh lúc 6 giờ chiều.
collocations: land safely, land on time, land at the airport

definition: The moment when an aircraft leaves the ground and becomes airborne.
example: Please fasten your seatbelt before takeoff.
exampleVi: Vui lòng thắt dây an toàn trước khi máy bay cất cánh.
collocations: before takeoff, takeoff time, delayed takeoff

definition: A person who is trained and licensed to fly an aircraft.
example: The pilot announced that the flight would be delayed by an hour.
exampleVi: Phi công thông báo rằng chuyến bay sẽ bị trì hoãn một giờ.
collocations: airline pilot, co-pilot, experienced pilot

definition: The group of people who operate and staff an aircraft, including pilots and flight attendants.
example: The cabin crew reminded passengers to fasten their seatbelts.
exampleVi: Phi hành đoàn nhắc nhở hành khách thắt dây an toàn.
collocations: cabin crew, flight crew, crew member

definition: The bags and suitcases a traveler carries or checks in for a trip.
example: Make sure your luggage doesn't exceed the weight limit.
exampleVi: Hãy đảm bảo hành lý của bạn không vượt quá giới hạn cân nặng cho phép.
collocations: carry-on luggage, checked luggage, luggage allowance

definition: A device used to weigh luggage at the check-in counter.
example: The agent placed the suitcase on the scale to check its weight.
exampleVi: Nhân viên đặt vali lên cân để kiểm tra trọng lượng.
collocations: weigh on the scale, baggage scale, check the scale

definition: Goods sold at an airport shop without the usual import tax.
example: She bought some perfume at the duty-free shop before boarding.
exampleVi: Cô ấy đã mua nước hoa ở cửa hàng miễn thuế trước khi lên máy bay.
collocations: duty-free shop, duty-free goods, buy duty-free

definition: An official spoken or written message informing passengers of something.
example: Listen carefully for the boarding announcement at your gate.
exampleVi: Hãy chú ý lắng nghe thông báo lên máy bay tại cổng của bạn.
collocations: make an announcement, boarding announcement, final announcement

definition: A moving conveyor belt in the baggage claim area that delivers checked luggage to passengers.
example: We waited by the carousel for our suitcases to appear.
exampleVi: Chúng tôi đứng đợi bên băng chuyền để lấy vali.
collocations: baggage carousel, wait at the carousel

definition: The act of a flight or passenger reaching a destination.
example: Please check the arrival board for your flight's status.
exampleVi: Vui lòng kiểm tra bảng thông báo chuyến bay đến để biết tình trạng chuyến bay của bạn.
collocations: arrival time, arrivals hall, on arrival

definition: The paved area of an airport where aircraft park, taxi, or take off and land.
example: The plane sat on the tarmac for an hour waiting for clearance.
exampleVi: Máy bay chờ trên sân đỗ trong một giờ để được cấp phép cất cánh.
collocations: wait on the tarmac, tarmac delay

definition: A form listing items a traveler must report to customs officials.
example: Fill out the customs declaration before you land.
exampleVi: Hãy điền vào tờ khai hải quan trước khi hạ cánh.
collocations: customs declaration, declaration form, fill out a declaration

definition: The process of settling a payment, such as at an airport shop or hotel.
example: There was a long line at the checkout in the duty-free shop.
exampleVi: Có một hàng dài chờ thanh toán ở cửa hàng miễn thuế.
collocations: checkout counter, checkout line, checkout time

definition: A comfortable waiting area in an airport, often reserved for business or first-class passengers.
example: Business class passengers can relax in the airport lounge before their flight.
exampleVi: Hành khách hạng thương gia có thể nghỉ ngơi tại phòng chờ sân bay trước chuyến bay.
collocations: airport lounge, VIP lounge, lounge access

definition: An official government document that certifies a person's identity and nationality for international travel.
example: Make sure your passport is valid for at least six months before you travel.
exampleVi: Hãy đảm bảo hộ chiếu của bạn còn hiệu lực ít nhất sáu tháng trước khi đi du lịch.
collocations: renew a passport, valid passport, passport control

definition: A large building where aircraft are kept, repaired, or maintained.
example: The plane was moved to the hangar for routine maintenance.
exampleVi: Máy bay được đưa vào nhà chứa để bảo trì định kỳ.
collocations: aircraft hangar, maintenance hangar, park in the hangar

definition: A large open area within an airport terminal used by passengers to walk between gates and facilities.
example: There are many shops and restaurants along the main concourse.
exampleVi: Có nhiều cửa hàng và nhà hàng dọc theo khu sảnh chính.
collocations: main concourse, terminal concourse, walk through the concourse

definition: A period of time spent waiting between connecting flights at an airport.
example: We had a three-hour layover in Singapore before our next flight.
exampleVi: Chúng tôi có ba giờ quá cảnh ở Singapore trước chuyến bay tiếp theo.
collocations: long layover, short layover, layover time

definition: The process of registering for a flight and checking in luggage before departure.
example: Online check-in opens 24 hours before the flight.
exampleVi: Thủ tục check-in trực tuyến mở 24 giờ trước chuyến bay.
collocations: online check-in, check-in counter, check-in time

definition: A brief stop at an airport during a longer journey, often lasting longer than a layover.
example: We planned a two-day stopover in Dubai on our way to London.
exampleVi: Chúng tôi đã lên kế hoạch dừng chân hai ngày ở Dubai trên đường đến London.
collocations: stopover flight, brief stopover, plan a stopover

definition: The act of a flight or passenger leaving an airport.
example: Check the departure board for your gate number.
exampleVi: Hãy kiểm tra bảng thông báo khởi hành để biết số cổng của bạn.
collocations: departure time, departure lounge, departure gate

definition: An official document or stamp that permits a person to enter a foreign country.
example: You need to apply for a visa before traveling to that country.
exampleVi: Bạn cần xin thị thực trước khi đến quốc gia đó.
collocations: apply for a visa, tourist visa, visa requirements

definition: The government checkpoint at an airport where officials verify travelers' passports and visas.
example: We waited in line at immigration for almost an hour.
exampleVi: Chúng tôi đã xếp hàng chờ ở khu vực nhập cảnh gần một giờ đồng hồ.
collocations: immigration officer, pass through immigration, immigration control

definition: A vehicle that transports passengers between an airport and nearby locations, such as hotels or parking lots.
example: The hotel offers a free shuttle service to and from the airport.
exampleVi: Khách sạn cung cấp dịch vụ xe đưa đón miễn phí đến và từ sân bay.
collocations: shuttle bus, airport shuttle, take the shuttle

definition: A female flight attendant (a member of the crew of an airplane who is responsible for the comfort and safety of its passengers).
example: We need to understand the concept of stewardess.
exampleVi: Chúng ta cần hiểu khái niệm tiếp viên hàng không.
collocations: flight attendant, senior stewardess

definition: A document that permits a passenger to board a flight, showing seat and gate information.
example: Please have your boarding pass and passport ready for inspection.
exampleVi: Vui lòng chuẩn bị sẵn thẻ lên máy bay và hộ chiếu để kiểm tra.
collocations: print a boarding pass, digital boarding pass, show your boarding pass

definition: The state or fact of being turbulent or agitated; tempestuousness, disturbance.
example: The plane shook due to severe turbulence over the mountains.
exampleVi: Máy bay rung lắc mạnh do nhiễu động không khí dữ dội phía trên dãy núi.
collocations: severe turbulence, hit turbulence, turbulence warning

definition: The airport structure from which air traffic controllers direct aircraft takeoffs and landings.
example: The pilot requested permission from the control tower before takeoff.
exampleVi: Phi công xin phép tháp điều khiển trước khi cất cánh.
collocations: air traffic control tower, contact the control tower
